data.title: chore: retire deprecated creator skills after skogai-routing is canonical
data.description:
## Problem

There are multiple overlapping skill-creation artifacts in the moved `skills/` tree, but current direction narrows their fate substantially:

* `skill-creator` is the official Claude/Codex skill creator and can be removed from this repo-local set for now

## Scope

* remove or archive `skill-creator` from the repo-local `skills/` set as a non-canonical imported duplicate
* remove or archive `skogai-skill-creator` as deprecated legacy setup
* review `skogai-skills` only for material worth folding into `skogai-routing` or later follow-on skills
* avoid preserving multiple competing entrypoints for skill creation once `skogai-routing` is established

## Done when

* deprecated creator variants are no longer presented as active repo-local entrypoints
* any valuable content from `skogai-skills` is either absorbed into the canonical routing-led design or explicitly archived
* the remaining skill-creation path follows from `skogai-routing` rather than parallel creator skills
* reviewers no longer have to guess which creator/routing skill is authoritative

updatedFrom.description:
## Problem

The current repo-local skill set contains overlapping authoring/router skills with no clear canonical boundary.

Main overlap clusters:

* `skill-creator`
* `skogai-skill-creator`
* `skogai-skills`
* `skogai-routing`

Concrete evidence:

* `skills/skogai-routing/workflows/` and `skills/skogai-skills/workflows/` share the same workflow set except for `claude-md-routing.md`
* `skills/skogai-routing/references/` and `skills/skogai-skills/references/` also substantially overlap
* `skill-creator` and `skogai-skill-creator` both cover skill creation but come from different conventions and tool assumptions

## Scope

* choose the canonical skill(s) for skill creation, skill review, and routing guidance
* merge or retire duplicate workflows/references where they do not carry distinct value
* preserve specialized content intentionally, especially the [CLAUDE.md](<http://CLAUDE.md>) routing material, rather than keeping accidental forks
* define how legacy or imported skill-creator content should be retained: canonical, compatibility alias, or archived reference

## Done when

* there is a clear answer to which skill to invoke for creating, auditing, and verifying skills
* duplicate workflow/reference trees are reduced or intentionally separated
* imported/legacy creator content is either integrated, aliased, or archived with rationale
* future skill work does not have to guess between near-duplicate entry points
